name: Chambers School Bus
principals: Chambers family (officer name not publicly disclosed)
districts_or_customers: Likely Northern Tioga / Troy Area / Athens Area / Canton Area school districts in Bradford & Tioga Counties PA (Columbia Cross Roads area); specific district contracts not confirmed
recent_news: FMCSA active since April 1998; clean inspection record (25 vehicle / 26 driver inspections in past 24 months, zero out-of-service violations, no crashes)
fit_notes: Independent rural PA school bus operator with ~38 buses and 32 drivers, intrastate-only, school students only - classic small family tuck-in profile for Summit in NEPA/Twin Tiers footprint. ~28-year-old company suggests possible succession opportunity.

name: Lech Transportation
principals: Lawrance Lech (President); related entity Raymond Lech also FMCSA-registered (DOT 773450)
districts_or_customers: Tamaqua, PA area; D&B lists ~2 employees and ~$165K revenue, suggesting very small subcontract or owner-operator scale; Tamaqua Area SD 2024-25 bus list does NOT show Lech as primary (lists Kistler and Felty) - Lech may be a subcontractor or serve neighboring Schuylkill County route
recent_news: No M&A or news found; D&B records show micro-business with unverified address/phone
fit_notes: Very small Tamaqua-area operator (2 employees per D&B) - likely owner-operator with 1-3 vehicles. Too small to be a meaningful tuck-in unless folded into a larger Schuylkill County roll-up. Source data (20 employees / $669K rev) appears inconsistent with D&B; verify scale before pursuit.

name: Agg School Transportation LLC
districts_or_customers: Hialeah / Miami-Dade area; likely Miami-Dade County Public Schools approved private bus contractor list (M-DCPS uses many small Hialeah-based contractors)
recent_news: No news, web presence, M&A, or Sunbiz detail confirmed via search; company appears to have minimal public footprint
fit_notes: Tiny Hialeah-based school bus contractor (~15 employees) in the highly fragmented Miami-Dade private school bus market. Could be a tuck-in in a South Florida roll-up but very small and unverified - low priority absent further sourcing.

name: Royal Transpo LLC
districts_or_customers: Evansville / Vanderburgh County IN area; listed on EVSC SECRW (Special Education Career Resource Workshop) transition resource fair as a Royal Transportation service provider operating 1st & 2nd shift transport and working with temp agencies in the Tri-State area
recent_news: Indiana LLC formed January 11, 2018 per OpenCorporates; no M&A activity found
fit_notes: Small Evansville-based provider; appears to mix special-needs/transition student transport with workforce/agency shuttle work rather than yellow-bus contracting. May not be a pure school bus contractor - more of a small non-CDL passenger/SPED van operator. Limited tuck-in fit for Summit unless paired with SPED expansion strategy.

name: Wilou Transit Svc Inc
districts_or_customers: Peekskill, NY area - potentially Peekskill City SD, Hendrick Hudson SD, Lakeland CSD, or Southern Westchester BOCES special-needs runs; no district contract confirmation found
recent_news: No news, M&A, or web presence found; only a thin Manta business directory listing exists
fit_notes: Very small Westchester County operator (~13 employees) with minimal digital footprint - could be a legacy SPED/small-van contractor in a high-cost, regulated NY market. Westchester is on the periphery of Summit's NY footprint; would need significantly more diligence to confirm it is even an active school transport operator.
